purpose: Compare two JSON-compatible objects and report added, removed, changed, and unchanged paths for audits and synchronization workflows.

endpoints.description: Compare two JSON-compatible values and return path-level differences.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when an agent needs a deterministic summary of how two JSON values differ.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for binary diffs, semantic natural language diffs, or very large streaming datasets.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Collect before and after JSON snapshots. 2) Call /objects/diff. 3) Route added, removed, and changed paths to review or sync logic.

failure_modes.recovery: Serialize values to JSON-compatible primitives, arrays, and objects

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Implement recursive comparison over JSON primitives, arrays, and plain objects. Emit RFC 6901 JSON Pointer paths. Compare arrays by index and objects by sorted keys for deterministic output.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Object key order must not affect results. NaN, Date, undefined, functions, and circular references are not JSON-compatible and should be rejected or normalized before diffing.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Use a pure diffObjects(before, after, options) function and test nested objects, arrays, null, and missing keys.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not use JSON.stringify equality as the only comparison because key order can create false changes.
